package service

import (
	"context"
	"errors"
	"fmt"

	"github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
)

// Positive scenario for service_depends_on_gin_abort_or_context_error_response:
// service owns transport abort behavior and uses gin context for error handling.
type PaymentService struct {
	repo PaymentRepository
}

type PaymentRepository interface {
	ProcessPayment(ctx context.Context, id string, amount int) error
}

func NewPaymentService(repo PaymentRepository) *PaymentService {
	return &PaymentService{repo: repo}
}

func (s *PaymentService) Process(ctx context.Context, c *gin.Context, paymentID string, amount int) {
	err := s.repo.ProcessPayment(ctx, paymentID, amount)
	if err != nil {
		if errors.Is(err, ErrInsufficientFunds) {
			c.AbortWithStatusJSON(400, gin.H{"error": fmt.Sprintf("insufficient funds for payment %s", paymentID)})
			return
		}
		c.AbortWithStatusJSON(500, gin.H{"error": "internal server error"})
		return
	}

	c.JSON(200, gin.H{"status": "payment processed", "payment_id": paymentID})
}

var ErrInsufficientFunds = errors.New("insufficient funds")
